name: Indianapolis (United States)

colors.symbolism: the cross and the central star
the central disc on which the star is set

description: A blue field crossed by a wide white Greek cross, with a red disc at the centre charged with a white five-pointed star.
history: Adopted on 28 May 1963, designed by Roger Gohl in conjunction with the Indianapolis sesquicentennial celebrations of 1971 (work having begun in the early 1960s).

features.symbolism: The white cross meeting at the centre symbolises Indianapolis as the 'Crossroads of America', with major highways and railroads converging on the city.
Indianapolis as the central point on which the highways converge.
Indianapolis as the capital of Indiana and as the focal point of the converging highways.
